item.title: No, it doesn't cost Anthropic $5k per Claude Code user
item.description: The viral claim that Anthropic loses $5,000 per Claude Code subscriber doesn't survive basic scrutiny. Let's do the actual maths.
item.author: (Martin Alderson)

item.title: Two Years of Emacs Solo: 35 Modules, Zero External Packages, and a Full Refactor | Rahul's Blog
item.description: Rahul's Blog

item.title: Home Remedies
Code is still a liability
item.description: The more code your team produces, the more code your team must maintain.

item.title: No, your six-year-old cannot paint this
item.description: On abstraction in art and software—why Picasso's childlike drawings take a lifetime to achieve, and what that has to do with LLMs.

item.title: Claude Tested Everything Except the One Thing That Mattered
item.description: Three weeks ago I wrote about building a social app in a week with Claude Code. The app shipped. My friends are using it. I kept building.

item.title: How I Run a Software Engineering Standup
item.description: I’ve been participating in software standups every day for over a decade, and over the past two years, leading them, too. Here’s how I run the best standup meetings that I can.

item.title: I Tried to Write a HTTP Service in Zig and Failed
item.description: I really like Zig. I have never wanted to write manually memory-managed code before. I don’t like C because it seems opaque and dangerous…

item.title: Formalizing Data Structures and Algorithms with Agents
item.description: Agents formalize imperative algorithms from natural language

item.description: Detailing my failures with localfirst frameworks

item.title: Reflections on vibe coding an iOS app
item.description: I am not an iOS developer, but a few days ago I published an iOS app called Pylo. Sure, I have been writing software professionally for nearly 20 years. I even wrote an iOS app before, but the late...

item.title: So you don't want to be a manager | Another Rodeo
item.description: Straight talk to a senior engineer wanting to stay on an IC track.

item.title: Every language should have a UUID type
item.description: I reach for UUIDs constantly. And yet in most languages, there's no standard type for them. You use a string, or a [16]byte, or you add a UUID package and ho...

item.title: Reflections on vibecoding ticket.el
item.description: It has now been a month since I started playing with Claude Code “for real” and by now I’ve mostly switched to Codex CLI: it is much snappier—who would imagine that a “Rewrite in Rust” would make things tangibly faster—and the answers feel more to-the-point than Claude’s to me.

item.title: Software got weird
item.description: Disjoint thoughts on AI and the future of building things.

item.title: Extremely Personal Software and the Collapse of the Talent Pipeline
item.description: Open source software is changing, and there's a new strain / a new breed of tooling: Extremely Personal Software (EPS). Skilled programmers will reap most of the benefits, while the market adjusts and people figure out how to bring this new workflow to the masses.

item.title: A New Chapter for Bluesky - Bluesky
item.description: After several intense and incredible years building Bluesky from the ground up, I've decided to step back as CEO and transition to a new role as Bluesky's Chief Innovation Officer.

item.title: Building a Procedural Hex Map with Wave Function Collapse
item.description: Procedural medieval islands from 4,100 hex tiles, built with WebGPU and a lot of backtracking.

item.title: GitHub - ruvnet/RuView: π RuView: WiFi DensePose turns commodity WiFi signals into real-time human pose estimation, vital sign monitoring, and presence detection — all without a single pixel of video.
item.description: π RuView: WiFi DensePose turns commodity WiFi signals into real-time human pose estimation, vital sign monitoring, and presence detection — all without a single pixel of video. - ruvnet/RuView

item.title: FontCrafter: Create Your Handwriting Font for Free
item.description: Convert your scanned handwriting into custom OTF, TTF, and WOFF2 fonts with local browser processing and vectorization. No account or uploads needed.
item.author: (Chris Pirillo)

item.title: Ireland shuts last coal plant, becomes 15th coal-free country in Europe
item.description: Moneypoint in County Clare, Ireland, joins the ranks of other European nations exiting coal by shutting off power generation at its sole remaining coal plant. Industry observers say Ireland’s increased renewable energy generation in recent years, particularly in wind, has contributed to this milestone. Moneypoint now functions as a backup oil burner under emergency instruction, but it is no longer active in the wholesale electricity market.
